key_risks: A sharp rotation out of crowded AI and semiconductor winners after their very large trailing gains could drive severe underperformance versus the S&P 500.
Hot June CPI or PPI data could push yields higher and compress valuations for long-duration growth and high-beta equities.
Taiwan or Korea country exposure could be hurt by adverse currency moves, export disappointments, or regional geopolitical headlines.
A broad risk-off move before the July 20 exit would disproportionately hit semiconductors, biotech, and emerging Asia exposures.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest recent and medium-term momentum in the universe, with AI-linked chip leadership likely to remain a near-term market focus into the July window.
High semiconductor supply-chain exposure with very strong trailing momentum, offering leveraged participation in the same AI hardware trend through country exposure.
Exceptional momentum and export/semiconductor sensitivity support continuation if global risk appetite and AI hardware demand remain firm.
Captures broad trend persistence across recent market leaders while diversifying away from a single industry or country vehicle.
Recent strength and high beta provide additional upside if risk appetite broadens beyond mega-cap technology during the scoring month.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio emphasizes one-month continuation in the strongest visible leadership: semiconductors, AI-linked Asian exporters, and momentum factor exposure. Macro data show resilient growth and risk appetite, making high-beta equity leadership more attractive than defensive assets despite elevated inflation and rates.
rationale_summary: A concentrated pro-momentum, pro-risk allocation is expected to outperform the S&P 500 if recent AI/semiconductor leadership persists through the July close. Diversification across SMH, Taiwan, South Korea, MTUM, and biotech reduces single-ETF dependence while keeping high upside beta.
